poem:
**Last Step**

Fourteen years—  
a hem of sequins  
fraying at the edge of evening.  

She smiles  
before the judges,  
polite, a small clenched yes  
in her throat.  

Bronx girl,  
Venezuelan sun  
in her hair's many changes,  
turns from the ballroom—  
not leaving,  
only carrying it  
into other rooms.
prompt: You are the poet William Carlos Williams. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Strictly’s longest-serving female dancer, Karen Hauer, quits show after 14 years Strictly Come Dancing’s longest-serving female professional dancer, Karen Hauer, has quit the show after 14 years. In a video posted on Instagram, Hauer said it was “the right time to close this chapter and take on new projects in other areas I’m passionate about”. She continued: “Strictly completely changed my life, not only as a performer and a teacher but as a human being. I’ve had the privilege of meeting so many incredible people and brilliant celebrity partners who have become close friends and people I admire so much.” Hauer said she would “even miss standing in front of the judges. Can you believe that? Smiling politely while sometimes secretly disagreeing. It’s been an honour to share the ballroom with them”. Thanking her fans, she said: “I’m so grateful you watched me grow over the years and witnessed all of my different hair styles.” The Venezuelan-born dancer, who moved to New York City at the age of eight, became tearful as she said: “Who would have thought that a young girl from the Bronx would end up becoming the longest-serving female professional dancer on a British TV institution. “Strictly, will always be in my heart. I love you all.” Hauer first hit TV screens when she auditioned for the US reality show So You Think You Can Dance in 2009. She came to the UK in 2010, with the Latin stage show Burn the Floor, and made her first". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
